citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
An APHIS official observed a female black and white sheep (ear tag #0889) in a mixed lamb/sheep enclosure with
cloudiness of the entire surface of the left eye. The lower half of the eye was opaque white in color. The sheep was
observed squinting in the affected eye, which is a symptom of pain or discomfort. According to the licensee, this condition
was not observed prior to the inspection.
Ocular issues such as this can cause discomfort and progress to more severe conditions such as blindness or loss of the
eye if left untreated. The facility must ensure that adequate daily observations are performed on all animals and that
problems related to animal health, behavior, and well-being are conveyed to the attending veterinarian in a timely manner.
The attending veterinarian must be apprised of this health problem, and the facility must follow their recommendations.
To be corrected by 12pm EST on April 15, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
An APHIS official observed a female black and white sheep (ear tag #0889) in a mixed lamb/sheep enclosure with 
cloudiness of the entire surface of the left eye. The lower half of the eye was opaque white in color. The sheep was 
observed squinting in the affected eye, which is a symptom of pain or discomfort. According to the licensee, this condition 
was not observed prior to the inspection.  
 
Ocular issues such as this can cause discomfort and progress to more severe conditions such as blindness or loss of the 
eye if left untreated. The facility must ensure that adequate daily observations are performed on all animals and that 
problems related to animal health, behavior, and well-being are conveyed to the attending veterinarian in a timely manner. 
The attending veterinarian must be apprised of this health problem, and the facility must follow their recommendations.  
 
To be corrected by 12pm EST on April 15, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
